spec_match.rationale: Starts a real `getDisplayMedia`, reads `track.getSettings().screenPixelRatio` (the new field the spec adds), and surfaces the captured-surface pixel ratio. Direct hit on the spec's only API surface.

interactive.rationale: Working start-capture button + 4 listeners, with a feature-detect probe and a live readout of the new settings field.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises `read the captured screen's native resolution + scale factor`; the page does exactly that with a real capture.

edge_cases.rationale: Reads the value on success but doesn't show what happens on a 1x display (DPR=1, no savings), nor when the user picks a window vs screen vs tab (DPR may differ).

references.rationale: `see also` includes the chromestatus link and the mediacapture-screen-share spec.

design_system.rationale: 37 CSS variable references, no raw hex, no button override mismatch.

openQuestions.title: Cover the 1x-display branch explicitly
openQuestions.detail: On a 1x monitor `screenPixelRatio === 1` and there's no over-capture problem. Show that branch so authors don't add unnecessary downscaling code for users on non-HiDPI displays.

openQuestions.title: Compare DPR across surface types
openQuestions.detail: Tab, window, and screen captures can have different effective DPRs. After the user picks a surface, surface the chosen `displaySurface` alongside the pixel ratio so the relationship between surface type and DPR is documented.

openQuestions.title: Show the absent-feature fallback shape
openQuestions.detail: If the browser doesn't expose `screenPixelRatio`, the page should suggest the fallback (assume DPR=1 / use `window.devicePixelRatio`). Spell that out in the absent-feature branch of the probe.

summary: Solid headline read-only demo of the new `screenPixelRatio` field with a real capture, working probe, and proper references. Edge coverage would benefit from the 1x-display branch and the cross-surface-type comparison.
